Welcome everyone to the longer version of the diagnostic sale. It is a two page sale. Try to keep this as short and as easy as humanly possible. I think that you should print this out on double sided paper and put them on clipboards so that the person who is actually doing the sale can literally read the questions. So the intention of the sales to make this as scalable as possible and as easy as possible to delegate. I know that Dylan already handed this over to a salesperson and they closed like a $2400 sale in their first try who had never sold before. So if you don't overthink this, it is a very, very, very simple process. And so you're literally just going to ask the questions. The reason I set it up this way was with the little black dots is because I think with your eyes it's easier to kind of check off the questions that you've already asked. And then you can write answers in the little gap that's underneath and that way it looks like it's much more of an intake kind of questionnaire back and forth rather than you interrogating them or selling them. So I have the pre meeting checklist just to make sure that any of your people who are working with you have already done the, they filled out the pre-framed questionnaire. They noted their bad habit cost section, right? Making sure that we've covered any of the costs that their bad habits might be costing them. So we can use those at the end to get them, you know, overcome price issues, show them before and afters and then get scale and body fat stuff. Now what changed? Why today? Why not yesterday? Or last week? Right? Or last month? Whatever. And so then they'd be like, well, you know, and then they may tell you that something happened, their kid said something, those are great. It's when you get those ones. But it'd be like, you know, it's been a while and I've been meaning to do this and I need to do it. Right? And so then that, they basically tell you the urgency so they can get around the, I need to think about it later. So this is all we're doing is we're overcoming the overcomes that would happen later. We're just doing it at the front of the set. All right, so what brought you today? What's the problem with your goal? They tell you your goal, great. So your goal is you're trying to lose weight, tone up, gain muscle, you're currently X, you're trying to get to Y, great, we do this all the time. Why now? What changed? Why today? Okay, understand. So why is this important to you? Right? Well, like what is not being in shape, preventing you from doing right now that you want to do? All right, which is the second question. They're missing fitness, they're missing nutrition, they're missing any kind of body. Which basically just means they didn't know what they were doing or they didn't stick to the thing they were doing, right? Right. Okay, cool. And what you can do is you can tell them the kind of story that goes along with each of them. So here it's a sustainable fitness is like, ah, you couldn't stick with the fitness part. Okay, got it. So, you know, a lot of times when people try to do fitness, they start things and they don't like the thing, which is why they don't keep doing it, right? Like we only do the things that we enjoy doing. Like you've never been like, man, I can't, do you watch Netflix, you watch your TV shows? And they'll probably say yes, because most humans do, you're like, got it. So you never think like, oh man, like I just can't get motivated to sit down and watch House of Cards. Like I just, I try to get motivated and I can't do it. But if Christian would say that, because you like House of Cards and we can get exercised to be the same thing, we just have to figure out which things you like the most. Does that make sense? Great, so you didn't have something that was really sustainable for you in personalized. Does that make sense? Great. Okay. If they say it was nutrition that was the issue and it might have been all three of these things, you don't know, but if nutrition was the issue, because that's what they try, you can tell an insurance story, which is like, having an accountability coach is just to make sure, it's the insurance. That way you know that you're gonna stick with it. It's fall off, the insurance policy covers you, and gets you right back on the road. That's what it's there for. And that's why the best athletes in the world still have coaches. And so it's the biggest lie in the world that people think that they're not gonna have a coach. Or they think that they don't need a coach when the reality is that every single person who achieves has a coach. And that's why you weren't able to stick with the past. Because no one checked in, of course you weren't. What happens when you plateau? You don't know. That's why it's good to have somebody who's already done this 100 times. Does that make sense? Okay, great. So ideal outcome, 12 months from now, what would make this the best decision in your time? What would have to happen? Right, now we're future-based. I tell you, we're like, got it. Okay, Susan, I think we can help to get there in 33 weeks. And assuming your goal is to keep it off, it'll take another, usually just cut the time period in half. So it'll take another 16 weeks to maintain, to get your body to adjust. Okay, now that's for a big, that's when we start to lose a lot of weight. But if someone's trying to lose like 30 pounds, they're like, awesome. Realistically, if you're trying to lose 30 pounds, this can take 15 to 20 weeks. And then probably another 10 weeks to maintain. So you won't need to do anything else after this. You need off 600. So it'd be 2300. It'd be 2300 today. And that'll literally, we'll solve the problem. We're done. Okay. Fair enough. I like this close instead of saying like great deal because people might not agree. It's a great deal. But if you say, sound fair, fair enough, then they have to agree. Yeah, that sounds fair. Right. It's not saying, Hey, this is a smoking deal. It's, yes, it sounds fair. So we're actually lowering the bar to get them to say yes. It's really like, great. You have your ID on you. Boom. And you go right into the clothes. Right. You're just like, great. You have your ID, great. You have your card and start filling out the contract form. That is it. Beyond that, the step down is you can give them 10% off. If they do half down, it's like, no worries. If you want, you can do 10% off. You can do half down today. You're gonna do that. Great. And then you go for, you know, whatever half of the, if it was 30, if it was 2900, then it would be 290 off. So that'd be 2,600. And then you say half of that would be 1,300. So it's like, cool. So can you do 1,300 today instead of 2,300? All right, great. Let's do that. And then after that, only weekly payments are gonna be, now you have the other 1,300 and you spread that over 30 weeks. So if you're looking at 1,300 over 30 weeks, right? And I'm gonna explain the math on that one one more time. Just for everyone. So if someone does the same day discount of 20%, right? So we said they had 30 weeks times 99 a week, right? So that's 29, 7, that's where the math came from. They want 20%, they do the painful discount you do times 0.8. This is gonna be 2376. That's what it is, painful to death. 30 weeks, right? So then it's like, cool. Then it's 44 bucks a week after that. Sound fair? And so what it is, all this does is it maximizes the upfront revenue that we're able to generate so that we can maximally acquire customers in a profitable sense, okay? And the thing is, is you gotta understand the numbers behind this because one out of five people is gonna be a whale. You're gonna be able to close a $3,000 ticket one out of five times, all right? And then one out of five times beyond that you're gonna close it. Let's just do 599 down to get you started and then we'll split the remainder of the duration you know, duration time. And so, and then at the end of this, if they're still like, I still can't do that, I'm out. Then you're like, cool, how about we just start you out for let's say eight weeks and then we'll go from there? Sound fair? Great. So then you can just close them on a shorter period of time. And then it's really just a question of like, what can you afford? I can afford $1,000 cold and let's just do, you know, we have to do at least $200 to start. That's just always how we've done it, which is also one of the best overhomes ever, which is just like, that's just always how we've done it and you'd be amazed how well that works. So you can either say 199 or 299. I'd recommend, I said 199. I'd recommend always getting 299 down. You can always get that from basically everyone. So just make that your bottom line. So 299 down. And then if you say it was a thousand bucks, then it's like $700 split over the next 10 weeks, right? So then it's gonna be 70 bucks a week for the next 10. Okay, great. And so after that, then you just have your simple, I was just like, do you wanna solve this for good and be done with it and not do all these cheap things that you've done in the past? Like, because you know that all that you're gonna do is come back here in six months or a year or three years, five, 10, 15, 20 pounds heavier, right? Right, okay, cool. And so that's basically negative future pacing. So it's like, cool, let's walk out the door today as though you don't sign up and see what your life looks like in a year with you gaining 20 more pounds. So you're 180 now, what if you're 200 a year from now? How much harder is it gonna be then, right? So the thing is, we know that you need these things, period, you need them. So now let's look at the bad habit stuff, let's look at how much money you're gonna save. Like, let's work on this, right? As long as it's not the first thing of like, you don't believe in me, you don't believe in the program, you don't think you can do it, whatever it is. Like, once you attack those things, then it's like, cool. Then let's make this work. And then you're working with them to close the sale. If it's a spouse thing, do they know that you wanna get in shape and fail? Yes, are they happy that you're not succeeding? No, of course not. So do you think it would be unreasonable to think that they would support you in doing something that would make you happy? No, so you're kind of using past logic to say that they're probably gonna be in support of you doing something if they know they don't like something else. Like, they don't approve of you being super unhappy, right? Of course not. So then why would they disapprove if you're doing something about it? Right, so that's kind of the whole point with the spouse close. And then you can also ask, what's their main concern? Because then it gets it away from them and then usually they'll use their husband as a foil as a dummy that they, right, it's reestablishing the value. And then mentioning the savings that go along with this. Like right now your current lifestyle is costing you more than this with. So you only save money while you're doing this. So I wouldn't make, money's never a concern, right? I would always say like money is never an issue because we're gonna save you so much money on all the other things that you're not gonna do, right? And then you can go into vote with our dollars. We vote with our dollars with the things we care about. So literally everything besides your rent, we can cover, you know what I mean? Like literally everything we can figure out if this is a priority for you, right? Because if it's not, then that's a totally different thing. But I know when you came in today, you said X, Y, and Z and I just wanna make sure that is that still, is that still how you feel? And they'll be like, yeah, you're right. And you're like, okay, now that's where rapport is really important for this. Now, just as an overarching question here, whenever you get into obstacles, whenever people start, you get into the fist fight, you have to remember that it's not combative. You cannot change your tone. If anything, you have to relax more. And it's kind of like in a fight for like people shortening their breath, they breathe too fast, they get too tense, right? When they're adrenaline spikes. So this is where practice comes into it. But the more you get into these sales, you have to get more childlike, more curious when you ask these questions. So, no, no way. What's your main concern? Totally caring. Not like, well, I mean, you're gonna have to do this anyways. Like why are you, like it's not a Q's, so you'll never close them. We include the service guarantee, which means that when they hit the goal that we will service them for that X period of time, provided they do X, Y, and Z. If they don't do X, Y, and Z, then the guarantee is void. That's not on you anymore, right? But if someone does all of those things and takes the supplements through that period of time, the reason you do the service guarantee is because if you are servicing someone past that guarantee and they're still on the supplements, you're still getting paid to service them. Okay, so that's a key point. And most people, after 30 weeks, if they've done a great job and literally had done all that stuff, it might take you an extra month to hit there. But that's gonna be a very, very, very, very, very, very small minority of cases. And be realistic with the goals that you're setting. Like be realistic, you're gonna guarantee if you're realistic. Give them two pounds a week and that's it. Like that's all you gotta do. Okay, so I hope that makes sense. That is the diagnostic sale. The longer version. Cole, my sales manager, who turned around a lot of 24s, made a second. We'll kind of get this one down and really understand some of these questions. Because now, this is where the scale of the salesman comes in. Sometimes, ask the questions, it'll lead them naturally to the conclusion. And with this kind of price anchoring, we'll be able to close way more at way higher ticket, which is the entire objective of this selling script. So, use wisely.
